notes: Deprecated broad source record retained for history. Do not use it for curriculum claims; use the separate normative Course of Study and interpretive Information commentary records with exact locators.
title: MEXT Curriculum Guidelines and Commentary

issuer: Agency for Cultural Affairs, Government of Japan
notes: Primary public guidance for bounded source-reading activities about using copyrighted works. Use printed pages 47-50 for the decision sequence before use, printed pages 74-77 for quotation and source indication, and printed pages 77-79 for the school-use boundary. The lesson's checklist, scenarios, and assessments are project-authored. Do not infer a legal conclusion for a specific use without checking the current law, contracts, facts, applicable exceptions, and other rights.
title: 著作権テキスト -令和7年度版-

notes: Official Japanese commons deed used to introduce the permissions and attribution conditions summarized for CC BY 4.0, including indicating changes and linking to the license. The deed is not the license text and does not resolve privacy, publicity, moral rights, or third-party material. Verify the actual material's license notice and current legal code before reuse.

issuer: Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology
notes: Interpretive general planning source, distinct from the normative Course of Study. Information I content areas begin on PDF pages 30, 34, 38, and 42. title: 高等学校学習指導要領（平成30年告示）解説 情報編

issuer: Personal Information Protection Commission, Government of Japan
notes: Primary legal guidance for bounded source-reading activities. Use section 3-1-1 on specifying purpose under Article 17(1) and section 3-4-2 on risk-based security control measures under Article 23. The guidance addresses personal information handling business operators; do not infer application or a legal conclusion for a classroom scenario without checking the specific facts, scope, current text, and exceptions. Broader lesson practices involving data minimization, secondary use, deletion planning, and re-identification risk are project-authored ethical and operational guidance unless a human reviewer confirms a more specific legal locator.
title: 個人情報の保護に関する法律についてのガイドライン（通則編）

notes: Use only Success Criteria 1.1.1 Non-text Content, 1.2.1 Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded), 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence, 1.4.1 Use of Color, 1.4.10 Reflow, 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.3 Focus Order, 2.4.7 Focus Visible, and 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Low-bandwidth delivery, plain-language practices, and the repository's classroom test procedure are project-authored design choices, not WCAG requirements cited through this record. A single self-test does not demonstrate WCAG conformance or representative accessibility. This record and its references make no WCAG conformance claim.
title: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2

notes: Python 3.10 language reference for assignment statements. Use for general claims about binding or rebinding names, not for deterministic exercise outputs. The executable examples are separately tested with the repository's locked Python runtime.
title: Python 3.10 Language Reference: Assignment statements

notes: Python 3.10 language reference for conditionals, loops, and function definitions. Cite only claims within the relevant statement sections; executable examples are separately tested with the repository's locked Python runtime.
title: Python 3.10 Language Reference: Compound statements

notes: Python 3.10 library reference for common sequence operations and list behavior, including list append. Use for general Python sequence claims, not for computed outputs; executable examples are separately tested with the repository's locked Python runtime.

notes: Python 3.10 tutorial section for defining and calling functions. Use for introductory function claims and retain the language reference for normative syntax details; executable examples are separately tested with the repository's locked Python runtime.

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
notes: Use only to support the general meaning of storage as retrievable retention of data. The glossary identifies NIST SP 800-88 Rev. 2, Appendix A, storage (PDF page 42; printed page 34) as its source and warns that each term-definition pair must be understood in the source document's context. Do not use this source for power-loss behavior, memory-capacity calculations, representation limits, or lesson-model outputs.
title: NIST CSRC Glossary: Storage

notes: Primary technical source for the layered Internet host model and general packet communication roles. Use Sections 1.1.2 and 1.1.3 only for bounded architecture claims, and consult the RFC Editor status page's current updates and related-document chain before any normative use. The classroom topology, sequence, examples, and assessments are project-authored.
title: RFC 1122: Requirements for Internet Hosts - Communication Layers

notes: W3C Recommendation used for bounded Web architecture concepts such as resource identification and interaction. It does not prescribe the project's service-flow model, lesson sequence, or evaluation criteria.
title: Architecture of the World Wide Web, Volume One

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
notes: NIST CSWP 29 supports bounded discussion of cybersecurity risk management, the CSF Core functions, and high-level outcomes. It is not used as a definition source for every security term and does not establish compliance, certification, legal applicability, or security assurance for any scenario. The lesson's controls and classroom examples are project-authored.
title: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0

notes: SQLite documentation snapshot accessed on 2026-07-15 for the lesson's bounded SELECT, WHERE, GROUP BY, aggregate, and ORDER BY behavior. Executable query results are separately checked with the repository's locked SQLite runtime. Do not infer full standard-SQL portability or use this source as evidence for data quality.
title: SQLite Documentation: SELECT

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
notes: Use only for the bounded convention that a byte consists of eight bits. Do not use this glossary entry for encoding, compression, storage-capacity, or lesson-model outputs.
title: NIST CSRC Glossary: Byte

notes: Python 3.10 library reference for mapping types and dictionary behavior. Use only for bounded Python dictionary claims; executable examples are separately tested with the repository's locked Python runtime.
title: Python 3.10 Standard Library: Mapping Types — dict

notes: Python 3.10 library reference for built-in functions, including int. Use only for bounded Python built-in behavior; executable conversions are separately tested with the repository's locked Python runtime.
title: Python 3.10 Standard Library: Built-in Functions

notes: Python 3.10 library reference for built-in constants, including None. In the data-cleaning lesson, using None as the missing-value marker is a project-authored modeling choice, not a general requirement.
title: Python 3.10 Standard Library: Built-in Constants

notes: Official trademark policy used only for the Python trademark and non-affiliation notice. It does not imply endorsement, sponsorship, or review of this project by the Python Software Foundation.
title: PSF Trademark Usage Policy

notes: Primary technical source used only to establish that 192.0.2.0/24 is reserved for documentation examples. The lesson does not use this record for subnetting, private-address behavior, routing policy, or operational configuration, and the example address is not a real service endpoint.
title: RFC 5737: IPv4 Address Blocks Reserved for Documentation

notes: Primary technical source for bounded HTTP semantics and the http and https URI schemes. Use Sections 3, 4, 6, and 15 only for resource, URI, client-server, request-response, and status concepts. The lesson's URL, service path, diagnostic cases, and assessment wording are project-authored; this record does not establish implementation conformance or service correctness.
title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics

notes: Current Best Current Practice terminology source for bounded DNS naming, resolution, and query-response concepts. It obsoletes RFC 8499. The lesson deliberately simplifies resolver behavior and does not teach DNS operation, configuration, cache policy, or security assurance through this record.
title: RFC 9499: DNS Terminology

notes: Current TLS 1.3 Standards Track source, which obsoletes RFC 8446. Use the Introduction and bounded certificate-handshake sections only for server authentication, confidentiality, integrity, reliable ordered transport, and certificate use. The lesson does not imply that TLS proves content truth, endpoint safety, authorization, or complete system security, and it does not prescribe cipher suites or configuration.
title: RFC 9846: The Transport Layer Security (TLS) Protocol Version 1.3

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
notes: Primary digital-identity guidance used for bounded authentication, authenticator-factor, MFA, and phishing-resistance concepts. This July 2025 final supersedes SP 800-63B. Its federal assurance requirements are not presented as universal classroom or organizational requirements, and the lesson does not imply that every MFA method is phishing-resistant or sufficient by itself.
title: NIST SP 800-63B-4: Digital Identity Guidelines - Authentication and Authenticator Management

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
notes: Primary standard used only for the bounded concept that hash algorithms produce message digests that can support change detection. NIST has announced a planned revision, so this record is not used to recommend algorithms, parameters, password storage, or implementation choices. The classroom comparison and examples are project-authored.
title: FIPS 180-4: Secure Hash Standard (SHS)

issuer: National Institute of Standards and Technology
notes: Primary standard used only for the bounded concept that digital signatures support detecting unauthorized data modification and authenticating the signatory. Consult the current NIST errata before technical use. This record does not establish content truth, legal non-repudiation, algorithm selection, implementation correctness, or organizational compliance.
title: FIPS 186-5: Digital Signature Standard (DSS)

notes: Primary source used only for the reservation of .test and related example DNS names. RFC 2606 is updated by RFC 6761; consult the current update chain before technical use. The lesson's host name and service are synthetic, and this record does not support DNS resolution behavior, service availability, or certificate validity.
title: RFC 2606: Reserved Top Level DNS Names

notes: Primary generic-syntax source used only for bounded URI component concepts such as scheme, authority or host, path, and query. The lesson uses URL as a classroom operational label for the synthetic https URI; it does not claim that RFC 3986 defines every current browser URL-parsing behavior or resolve the broader terminology history.
title: RFC 3986: Uniform Resource Identifier (URI): Generic Syntax

notes: Current TCP base specification used only for the bounded reliable, in-order byte-stream and retransmission concepts in the lesson's TCP example. It updates RFC 1122 in its TCP scope. The lesson does not imply that every transport protocol provides the same properties and does not teach TCP implementation, tuning, ports, or packet analysis.
title: RFC 9293: Transmission Control Protocol (TCP)

notes: Current Standards Track source for representing and verifying application-service identity in TLS; it obsoletes RFC 6125. Use Sections 1, 2, and 6 only for bounded matching of a client's reference identity to identifiers presented in a server certificate. This record does not cover identity proofing of people, certificate-chain validation, content truth, service authorization, or complete endpoint safety.
title: RFC 9525: Service Identity in TLS
